data.narrative:
Rocket Lab is a publicly traded Space & Aerospace company. The peer universe consists of 3 same-sector, same-stage comparables: Planet Labs ($7.0B), Firefly Aerospace ($6.3B), and Astroscale ($900M).

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach here. The median valuation across these three public Space & Aerospace peers is $6.3B, with a range spanning $900M at the low end to $7.0B at the high end. Public-comp anchoring via revenue multiples was not feasible; while the broader Space & Aerospace sector trades at a 64.2x price-to-sales median across 6 public tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL), Rocket Lab's revenue is not disclosed in the data set, precluding P/S application.

The triangulated valuation range derived from peer-median analysis is $5.4B to $7.2B, with a median point of $6.3B. This analysis rests on a single method and a limited peer set of three firms. The primary uncertainty is the wide dispersion within the peer group—Astroscale at $900M represents a material outlier relative to Planet Labs and Firefly. Additional sensitivity analysis anchored to revenue or profitability metrics, if available, would narrow the range and increase confidence in the median estimate.
